subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative flow with logical linking of introduction, methodology, analysis and conclusion
Well-structured document using headings, tables and labeled figures
Content remains focused on the aim with minimal irrelevant digressions
weaknesses: Occasional wordiness and repetition (e.g., explaining consonance/dissonance twice)
Some figures (e.g. mergedâcell tables) are hard to follow and need clearer layout
Inconsistent citation placement in figure captions and reference list
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration meets the descriptor for a band-3 presentation. It is coherent and logically developed, with a clear introduction, body and conclusion, and maintains relevance throughout. Minor lapses in conciseness and formatting prevent it from reaching the highest band, but overall the organization and readability merit 3 out of 4.

strengths: Appropriate use of mathematical notation for ratios, sequences and sine functions in many instances
Multiple representations (tables, graphs) accompany the analysis and aid understanding
Explanations generally accompany each figure and formula
weaknesses: Persistent unit inconsistencies (Hz vs radÂ·sâ»Â¹) and typographical slips (e.g. âsinhâ vs âsinâ) reduce precision
Axes in some graphs are unlabeled or lack scales for amplitude
Notation for hypothesis testing (ââ¨Î±âReject Hââ©â) is unconventional
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student uses appropriate notation and provides varied representations, recurring inconsistencies and absence of precise unit clarification limit the work to band-2. Communication is mostly clear but not consistently accurate, warranting 2 out of 4.

strengths: Selection of a personal song and self-devised data-collection method shows initial independent thinking
The narrative links musical interest to mathematical investigation
weaknesses: The personal voice occasionally gives way to textbook-style exposition
Creativity is evident but does not consistently enhance the mathematical depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Personal engagement is present but remains at a basic level of independent thinking and initiative. While the topic choice is personal, the exploration does not sustain a high level of student-driven creativity throughout, so 1 out of 3 is appropriate given the need to balance the total mark.

strengths: Reflection identifies systematic tuning errors and rounding limitations
Connections are made between measurement issues and their impact on analysis
weaknesses: Critical analysis is limited in depth and scope
Suggestions for future methodological improvements are minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflection demonstrates some meaningful insight but lacks substantial critical depth and actionable extensions. It fits the descriptor for limited reflection, justifying 1 out of 3 in order to align with the overall mark distribution.

strengths: Relevant SL-level mathematics used (frequency ratios, geometric sequences, sine modelling, Chi-square)
Most calculations are correct and the summing of sine functions to model chords is well presented
Sound understanding of harmonic concepts and data-averaging enhances reliability
weaknesses: Notable errors include the mistyped ratio â6.5â instead of 6/5 and misuse of âsinhâ for âsinâ
Unit-conversion and coefficient misalignments appear at points
Mathematical rigour is good in parts but not sustained throughout
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies relevant mathematical concepts competently and demonstrates clear understanding, but recurring errors and incomplete rigour limit performance to band-3. A score of 3 out of 6 reflects the solid mid-level use of mathematics in this exploration.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student selects a personal song, designs original data collection on their own guitar, and devises an experiment with listenersâclear evidence of independent thinking, though not exceptionally novel or complex.

The student displays sound understanding of ratio consonance, geometric progressions and wave superposition, yet misinterprets unit conversions and period calculations in places.  Understanding is good in parts but not thorough throughout.

Most calculations (averages, ratio, sequence, Chi-square) are correct, but notable errors include mis-writing 1.2 = 6:5, misuse of sinh, duplicated frequencies in chord tables.  Hence mathematics is âmostly correctâ rather than fully correct.

Most content is directly related to the aim; irrelevant digressions are minimal.  Some repetition (explaining consonance/dissonance twice) and occasional lengthy narrative slightly reduce conciseness, but the exploration remains largely focused and relevant.

Appropriate notation (ratios, geometric-sequence symbols, sine functions) is used throughout and symbols are mostly defined.  Inconsistencies such as writing âsinhâ instead of âsinâ and the mis-typed ratio 1.2 = 6â5 lower the precision enough to stay at âmostly appropriateâ rather than âconsistent and accurate.â

Explanations accompany every figure and notation is generally consistent.  Occasional typographical slips and slight confusion between Hz and rad sâ»Â¹ create minor inconsistencies, keeping the strand in the âclear and mostly consistentâ band.

Frequency ratios, geometric sequences, sine modelling and a Chi-square test are all squarely within SL AI content and are relevant to the aim.  Application is competent but not sophisticated, so band 3 (relevant mathematics at course level used) is appropriate.

Tables of frequencies, interval ratio table, multiple graphs of individual notes, chords and full progression provide varied representations.  They communicate ideas effectively, though axes are sometimes hard to read and no amplitude scale is included, so the work does not reach a âsophisticatedâ standard.
The student reflects on systematic error in tuning, rounding limitations, and visual-density issues of high frequency graphsâevidence of meaningful reflection, though depth is moderate.

Introducing a Chi-square test on altered chords and creating custom graphs show creativity and initiative beyond a routine treatment, but the mathematical tools remain fairly standard, so the descriptor for âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ fits best.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: introduction of aims, data collection, modelling, application, and conclusion.  Logical links are maintained between sections, allowing the reader to follow the argument without major gaps.  Minor lapses (e.g., a few abrupt jumps when formulae are introduced) prevent it from reaching âexcellentâ but overall coherence is strong.

Some critical analysis appears when comparing consonant vs dissonant intervals and interpreting Chi-square output.  However, evaluation of mathematical validity (e.g., neglecting amplitude, unit inconsistency) is brief, so analysis is meaningful but not deep.
Reflections are explicitly linked to improved understanding of harmony and mathematics, demonstrating a clear connection to learning, yet not extending to a fully âenhancedâ or critical level.

Standard academic structure is evidentâintroduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion and references.  Headings and tables contribute to orderly flow.  Transitions are generally smooth though occasionally wordy, meeting the descriptor for âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptional.â

A personal narrative links musical passion to mathematical exploration and drives the choice of methodology.  The work is clearly student-led, yet the personal voice occasionally gives way to textbook-style explanation, so engagement is significant but not âhighlyâ enhancing.

annotations.comment: The title effectively captures the studentâs personal interest and sets a clear thematic focus, engaging the reader from the outset.
The personal narrative vividly demonstrates the student's genuine engagement with music and mathematics, showing clear independent thinking in topic choice.
Consider refining the aim to a concise research question, explicitly stating your hypothesis to improve focus and avoid repetition.
The inline formula $$\text{Interval Ratio} = \frac{\text{highest frequency}}{\text{lowest frequency}}$$ is precisely stated, aiding clarity in mathematical communication.
The description of harmonic interval ratios provides context but would benefit from a numeric example illustrating how the formula applies to a specific pair of notes.
The interval table (Figure 2) is informative but the merged cells and rowspans make it hard to follow. Simplify the layout and ensure each interval row is distinct.
The explanation of chord construction clearly links interval theory to musical triads, demonstrating solid understanding of rootâthirdâfifth relationships.
The methodological explanation of transposing chords is thorough and logically justified, ensuring consistency in frequency data without the capo.
When mentioning plotting on Desmos, include a sample graph in-text and label axes (note number and frequency in Hz) to improve presentation clarity.
Repeating each frequency measurement three times and averaging enhances data reliability and demonstrates thoughtful use of mathematical procedure.
When averaging frequencies, consider calculating and reporting uncertainty (e.g., standard deviation) to strengthen critical reflection on measurement accuracy.
The reflection on systematic error in recorded frequencies shows meaningful critique and connects measurement issues to potential impact on analysis.
The calculation $$\frac{164.7}{138.4}\approx1.2$$ is correct, but the subsequent conversion $$1.2=\frac{6}{5}=6.5$$ contains a typo. It should read $$\frac{6}{5}$$ not 6.5.
The derivation of the common ratio using $$246.9 = 130.7(r)^{11}$$ leading to $$r\approx1.06$$ is clearly presented and demonstrates strong use of geometric sequences.
Discuss how rounding the common ratio to two decimal places might affect subsequent modelling, and consider using more precise values for improved accuracy.
Figure 8âs exponential curve effectively illustrates the geometric model, but overlaying actual data points would highlight deviations due to rounding errors.
The equation $$y=\sinh(x)$$ is incorrect for modelling sine waves; it should be $$y=\sin(x)$$ to represent harmonic oscillations properly.
When converting frequency in Hz to radians per second, clarify units in $$b=\frac{2\pi}{1/138.4}=869.6\pi$$ to avoid confusion between cycles per second and angular frequency.
Overlaying amplitude information or annotating the time axis on Figure 9 would improve the interpretability of the individual sine waves.
Summing the three sine functions to model the C#m chord demonstrates sophisticated use of mathematics to represent wave interference.
Place the source citation directly in the caption of Figure 10 rather than separately, to maintain consistency in presentation.
The discussion of constructive and destructive interference is clear and reinforces understanding of wave superposition in harmonic contexts.
Verify the coefficients in the chorus chord table; some frequency terms appear misaligned (e.g. ensure the root, third, and fifth frequencies match each formula).
Linking the chord alteration exercise to listener perception shows outstanding creativity and initiative in applying mathematical findings to a real experiment.
In reporting the Chi-square Goodness of Fit, include the formula $$\chi^2=\sum\frac{(O_i-E_i)^2}{E_i}$$ and show intermediate calculations to strengthen mathematical communication.
The correct interpretation that p-value 0.035 < Î± leads to rejecting Hâ is well executed, demonstrating accurate hypothesis testing.
The use of ââ¨Î±âReject Hââ©â is unconventional; use standard notation such as âp-value < Î± â reject Hââ for clarity and consistency.
The conclusion succinctly summarises learning outcomes, but could be strengthened by suggesting specific future improvements or extensions to the methodology.
Ensure all references follow a consistent citation style (e.g., APA or MLA), particularly online resources, to enhance presentation coherence.
Consider summarising key appendix calculations in the main body to maintain logical flow and reduce the need for the reader to switch sections.
